---
name: ddx
description: Operates the DDx toolkit for document-driven development. Covers beads (work items), the queue, executions, agents, harnesses, personas, reviews, spec-id. Use when the user says "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "review this", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", "check against spec", "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "what's blocking the queue", "create a bead", "file this as work", "run an agent", "dispatch", "use a persona", "how am I doing", "ddx doctor", or mentions any ddx CLI command.
---

# DDx

DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is a CLI platform for
document-driven development. It ships a bead tracker (portable work
items with acceptance criteria), a task-execution boundary (DDx
forwards raw passthrough constraints for harness, provider, model,
and profile while power travels as `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds;
Fizeau owns concrete routing, provider/model discovery, aliases,
fuzzy matching, catalog lookups, and transcript/session rendering), a
persona system (bindable AI personalities), a library registry
(plugins with prompts, templates, personas), and git-aware
synchronization. This skill makes any skills-compatible coding agent
(Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) understand and operate
the DDx surface correctly.

## How this skill works

The skill body you're reading is an **overview** plus an **intent
router**. The real domain guidance lives in `reference/*.md` files.

**Directive: before responding to any DDx-related request, read the
matching reference file from the router table below. The router is
not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's
guidance, not this overview alone.**

## Vocabulary

Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
exact definitions.

- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
- **Queue** — the set of active beads. The *execution-ready queue* is
  `status=open` work whose dependencies are closed and which is not parked by
  cooldown, supersession, or execution-eligibility policy. `ddx bead ready`,
  `ddx bead status`.
- **Lifecycle status** — the persisted bead state: `proposed`, `open`,
  `in_progress`, `blocked`, `closed`, or `cancelled`.
- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
- **Blocked** — `status=blocked` work paused by a hard external recheckable
  blocker. Ordinary dependency waits are derived queue state, not blocked
  status. `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Proposed** — work awaiting operator decision before autonomous execution.
  Proposed beads are not worker-eligible until accepted, rewritten, split,
  blocked externally, cancelled, or otherwise resolved.
- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes
  `success` or `already_satisfied`.
- **Run** — one task invocation atom. `ddx run` calls Fizeau `Execute`
  once with prompt/config, `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds, and optional
  raw passthrough constraints.
- **Try** — one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. `ddx try <id>` wraps
  `ddx run` with bead prompt resolution, evidence capture, and merge/preserve
  finalization.
- **Work** — drain the bead queue. `ddx work` picks ready beads and invokes
  `ddx try`; it owns queue iteration and retry policy.
- **Worker** — a `ddx work` / `ddx try` process scoped to a specific project
  root. Do not call every `ddx work` process a "DDx worker" when multiple
  projects are visible. If the user says "DDx worker" without a qualifier,
  assume they mean the worker for the current/requested project. Say "worker
  for `<project>`" or "DDx-powered worker"; reserve "DDx repo worker" for work
  whose project root is the DDx repository.
- **Execution** — a generic DDx execution run (FEAT-010). Includes execution
  definitions, execution records, and execution evidence under `.ddx/runs/<id>/`.
- **Agent** — an AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
  invoked via a harness. Not a subagent (harness-specific — see
  below).
- **Harness** — a Fizeau routing concept. DDx may pass `--harness` as
  an operator-supplied constraint, but DDx does not validate, rank, fallback, or
  branch on harness names.
- **Persona** — a Markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) that
  defines an AI personality. DDx injects the body as a system-prompt
  addendum to `ddx run`. `ddx persona list/show/bind`.
- **Role** — an abstract function (e.g., `code-reviewer`,
  `test-engineer`) a workflow can reference. Projects bind roles to
  personas.
- **Binding** — a project-specific `role: persona` map in
  `.ddx/config.yaml` under `persona_bindings`.
- **Power bounds** — `MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` integers passed to the
  upstream execution service. DDx may raise `MinPower` on eligible retries;
  Fizeau owns concrete route selection within those bounds.
- **Plugin** — a self-contained extension installed to
  `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`. The default `ddx` plugin (personas,
  prompts, patterns, templates) is auto-installed by `ddx init`.
  `ddx install <name>`.
- **Skill** — an agentskills.io-standard directory (SKILL.md +
  optional `reference/`, `evals/`, `scripts/`). This `ddx` skill is
  the one DDx ships. Plugins can ship additional skills.
- **Subagent** — a harness-local concept for running a prompt in an
  isolated context (Claude Code's `.claude/agents/` + `context:
  fork`; Codex's `agents/`; others differ). DDx does not specify subagent
  orchestration; that remains harness business.
- **Update** — refresh plugin/toolkit content to a newer version.
  `ddx update [<plugin>]`.
- **Upgrade** — replace the DDx binary with a newer release.
  `ddx upgrade`.
- **Review** — two distinct concepts. **Bead review**
  (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a completed bead against its
  acceptance criteria. **Comparison/adversarial review** is a workflow skill
  composition over `ddx run`, not a core quorum flag. See `reference/review.md`.
- **Governing artifact** — the document that authorizes a bead's
  work: a FEAT-\*, SD-\*, TD-\*, or ADR-\* under `docs/`. Referenced
  via `spec-id`.
- **Spec-id** — the `spec-id: <ID>` custom field on a bead pointing
  at its governing artifact.

## Intent router

Before responding, read the matching file.

| User says / asks about | Read this file |
|---|---|
| "what should I work on next", "what's blocking the queue", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", broad queue orientation or planning | `reference/interactive.md` |
| write/plan work, "create a bead", "file this as work", bead metadata, acceptance criteria, dependencies | `reference/beads.md` |
| `ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead `<id>`", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "start the worker", "start N workers and watch", "monitor the drain", verify-and-close | `reference/work.md` |
| "review this", "check against spec", bead review, quorum review, code review, adversarial check | `reference/review.md` |
| "assess bead readiness", "score a bead", "triage a failed attempt", "refine a bead", bead authoring lint | `bead-lifecycle/` |
| "run an agent", "dispatch", harness/provider/model passthrough, power, effort, "use a persona", role bindings | `reference/agents.md` |
| "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "how am I doing", health check, "ddx doctor", sync status | `reference/status.md` |

If the intent spans multiple files (e.g., "create a bead and then
run it"), read beads.md first, then work.md. If no match, ask the
user which concept they mean rather than guessing.

## Top-level policy reminders

These apply across all DDx operations. Do not restate them in every
reference file; do not violate them.

- **Never edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.** All tracker changes go
  through `ddx bead create/update/close/dep`. Direct edits corrupt
  bead history and cannot be audited.
- **Tracker changes are commit-worthy.** After `ddx bead create`,
  `update`, `dep add/remove`, or `close`, commit the resulting
  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` change — either as a tracker-only commit or
  folded into the same commit as related implementation changes.
- **Preserve bead-attempt commit history.** Branches containing
  `ddx try` / `ddx work` execution commits carry an audit trail. **Never
  squash, rebase, filter, or amend** these commits. Use only
  `git merge --ff-only` or `git merge --no-ff` when merging.
  `gh pr merge --squash` and `--rebase` are forbidden on these
  branches.
- **Work in worktrees for parallel agents.** Use `wt switch -c
  <branch>` (worktrunk) or equivalent to give each concurrent agent
  its own isolated checkout. `ddx try` does this automatically;
  manual parallel work should too.
- **Power-first execution dispatch.** Default to `ddx run`/`ddx try`/`ddx work`
  with power bounds. `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are passthrough
  constraints only; DDx must not use them for routing policy.

## Links out

- Full CLI reference: `ddx --help`, `ddx <subcommand> --help`.
- Governing feature specs: see `FEAT-*` documents under your
  project's `docs/` tree — especially the CLI, beads, agent-service,
  executions, and skills features.
- Personas README: shipped by the default `ddx` plugin at
  `.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/README.md`.
- Open standard this skill conforms to:
  [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io).

data.text: The existing failure classifier is coarse, but the loop already threads detailed report fields through to events. I’m checking the `try`/executor boundary next so the signal-killed distinction lands in the same report path that already drives cooldown and disruption decisions.

func toTryReport(report ExecuteBeadReport) agenttry.Report {
	cycleTrace := make([]agenttry.ExecutionCycleTrace, 0, len(report.CycleTrace))
	for _, entry := range report.CycleTrace {
		cycleTrace = append(cycleTrace, toTryCycleTrace(entry))
	}
	return agenttry.Report{
		BeadID:                      report.BeadID,
		AttemptID:                   report.AttemptID,
		WorkerID:                    report.WorkerID,
		Harness:                     report.Harness,
		Provider:                    report.Provider,
		Model:                       report.Model,
		ActualPower:                 report.ActualPower,
		Status:                      report.Status,
		Detail:                      report.Detail,
		SessionID:                   report.SessionID,
		BaseRev:                     report.BaseRev,
		ResultRev:                   report.ResultRev,

		TargetBranch:                report.TargetBranch,
		EvidenceRev:                 report.EvidenceRev,
		ProjectRoot:                 report.ProjectRoot,
		PreserveRef:                 report.PreserveRef,
		RetryAfter:                  report.RetryAfter,
		NoChangesRationale:          report.NoChangesRationale,
		ReviewVerdict:               report.ReviewVerdict,
		ReviewRationale:             report.ReviewRationale,
		CycleTrace:                  cycleTrace,
		PowerClass:                  report.PowerClass,
		ProbeResult:                 report.ProbeResult,
		CostUSD:                     report.CostUSD,
		DurationMS:                  report.DurationMS,
		RequestedProfile:            report.RequestedProfile,
		RoutingIntentSource:         report.RoutingIntentSource,
		EstimatedDifficulty:         report.EstimatedDifficulty,
		InferredPowerClass:          report.InferredPowerClass,
		ResolvedPowerClass:          report.ResolvedPowerClass,
		EscalationCount:             report.EscalationCount,
		FinalPowerClass:             report.FinalPowerClass,
		DecompositionRecommendation: report.DecompositionRecommendation,
		DecompositionRationale:      report.DecompositionRationale,
		DecomposedChildIDs:          append([]string(nil), report.DecomposedChildIDs...),
		ExecutionDecision:           report.ExecutionDecision,
		Disrupted:                   report.Disrupted,
		DisruptionReason:            report.DisruptionReason,
		OutcomeReason:               report.OutcomeReason,
		ResourceExhausted:           report.ResourceExhausted,
		Error:                       report.Error,
		Stderr:                      report.Stderr,
		RateLimitBudget:             report.RateLimitBudget,
	}
}

func fromTryRateLimitRetryInfo(info agenttry.RateLimitRetryInfo) RateLimitRetryInfo {
	var result *Result
	if info.Report != nil {
		result = &Result{
			Harness:  info.Report.Harness,
			Provider: info.Report.Provider,
			Model:    info.Report.Model,
		}
	}
	return RateLimitRetryInfo{
		Attempt:    info.Attempt,
		Wait:       info.Wait,
		Source:     info.Source,
		Result:     result,
		Elapsed:    info.Elapsed,
		OverBudget: info.OverBudget,
	}
}

		},
		ActualRoute: agenttry.ExecutionCycleRouteFacts{
			Harness:         entry.ActualRoute.Harness,
			Provider:        entry.ActualRoute.Provider,
			Model:           entry.ActualRoute.Model,
			ActualPower:     entry.ActualRoute.ActualPower,

		},
		ReviewGroupID:   entry.ReviewGroupID,
		ReviewerIndices: append([]int(nil), entry.ReviewerIndices...),

		ReviewResult: agenttry.ExecutionCycleReviewResult{

		ReconcileStatus:      entry.ReconcileStatus,
		DecomposedChildIDs:   append([]string(nil), entry.DecomposedChildIDs...),
		ExecutionDecision:    entry.ExecutionDecision,
	}
}

func TestParseExecuteLoopFlags_AllFlagsPopulateSpec(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
	workCmd, _, err := root.Find([]string{"work"})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	setFlag := func(name, value string) {
		t.Helper()
		require.NoError(t, workCmd.Flags().Set(name, value))
	}
	setFlag("project", dir)
	setFlag("from", "HEAD~1")
	setFlag("harness", "claude")
	setFlag("model", "sonnet")
	setFlag("profile", "smart")
	setFlag("provider", "anthropic")
	setFlag("effort", "high")
	setFlag("ignore-cooldown", "true")
	setFlag("reason", "operator unblock")
	setFlag("watch", "true")
	setFlag("idle-interval", "45s")
	setFlag("json", "true")
	setFlag("local", "true")
	setFlag("no-review", "true")
	setFlag("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", "true")
	setFlag("review-harness", "codex")
	setFlag("review-model", "gpt-5.4")
	setFlag("max-cost", "12.5")
	setFlag("max-recovery-cost", "2.5")
	setFlag("preclaim-warn-threshold", "7")
	setFlag("preclaim-timeout", "45s")
	setFlag("request-timeout", "2m")
	setFlag("rate-limit-max-wait", "90s")
	setFlag("min-power", "7")
	setFlag("max-power", "8")

	spec, dispatch, err := parseExecuteLoopSpec(workCmd, true)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Equal(t, dir, spec.ProjectRoot)
	assert.Equal(t, "HEAD~1", spec.FromRev)
	assert.Equal(t, "claude", spec.Harness)
	assert.Equal(t, "sonnet", spec.Model)
	assert.Equal(t, "smart", spec.Profile)
	assert.Equal(t, "anthropic", spec.Provider)
	assert.Equal(t, "high", spec.Effort)
	assert.True(t, spec.IgnoreCooldown)
	assert.Equal(t, "operator unblock", spec.CooldownOverrideReason)
	assert.Equal(t, executeloop.ModeWatch, spec.Mode)
	assert.Equal(t, 45*time.Second, spec.IdleInterval.Duration)
	assert.True(t, spec.NoReview)
	assert.Equal(t, "codex", spec.ReviewHarness)
	assert.Equal(t, "gpt-5.4", spec.ReviewModel)
	assert.True(t, spec.OpaquePassthrough)
	assert.Equal(t, 12.5, spec.MaxCostUSD)
	assert.Equal(t, 2.5, spec.MaxRecoveryCostUSD)
	assert.Equal(t, 45*time.Second, spec.PreClaimTimeout.Duration)
	assert.Equal(t, 2*time.Minute, spec.RequestTimeout.Duration)
	assert.Equal(t, 90*time.Second, spec.RateLimitMaxWait.Duration)
	assert.Equal(t, 7, spec.MinPower)
	assert.Equal(t, 8, spec.MaxPower)
	assert.Equal(t, executeloop.SpecCurrentVersion, spec.SpecVersion)
	assert.Equal(t, "true", dispatch.JSON)
	assert.True(t, dispatch.Local)
}

func TestWork_IgnoreCooldownDrainsCooledQueue(t *testing.T) {
	env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
	store := bead.NewStore(env.Dir + "/.ddx")
	require.NoError(t, store.Init(context.Background()))

	future := time.Now().UTC().Add(6 * time.Hour)
	for _, tc := range []struct {
		id       string
		priority int
	}{
		{id: "cool-4", priority: 4},
		{id: "cool-1", priority: 1},
		{id: "cool-3", priority: 3},
		{id: "cool-0", priority: 0},
		{id: "cool-2", priority: 2},
	} {
		require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), &bead.Bead{
			ID:       tc.id,
			Title:    "Cooled " + tc.id,
			Priority: tc.priority,
		}))
		require.NoError(t, store.SetExecutionCooldown(tc.id, future, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges, "retry later", "base-rev-1"))
	}

	var got []string
	factory := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir)
	factory.AgentRunnerOverride = &tryHookRunnerStub{t: t}
	factory.tryExecutorOverride = agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
		got = append(got, beadID)
		return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
			BeadID:    beadID,
			Status:    agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
			ResultRev: "rev-" + beadID,
		}, nil
	})

	out, err := executeCommand(
		factory.NewRootCommand(),
		"work",
		"--ignore-cooldown",
		"--reason", "operator unblock",
		"--no-review",
		"--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing",
	)
	require.NoError(t, err, "ddx work must drain cooled beads when override is explicit: %s", out)
	assert.Equal(t, []string{"cool-0", "cool-1", "cool-2", "cool-3", "cool-4"}, got)
}

// TestWork_NetworkFreeDrain_NoFetchInLoop verifies reliability principle P9:
// the drain loop's pre-claim hook never blocks on network I/O. The fixture
// repo has an origin remote whose URL points at a deleted path; the last
// observed refs/remotes/origin/main remains as a cached tip. A network-free
// drain must claim and execute a ready bead without error even though no
// `git fetch` against origin could succeed.

package cmd

)

// TestWorkCommandHasPassthroughFlags verifies that ddx work exposes the
// harness/provider/model/min-power/max-power passthrough flags that operators
// use to constrain agent routing. These flags are forwarded opaquely; ddx work
// does not validate or branch on their string values.
func TestWorkCommandHasPassthroughFlags(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()

	workCmd, _, err := root.Find([]string{"work"})
	require.NoError(t, err, "ddx work must exist")
	require.NotNil(t, workCmd)

	for _, name := range []string{"harness", "provider", "model", "min-power", "max-power", "claim-rate-window", "claim-rate-threshold", "preclaim-warn-threshold"} {
		f := workCmd.Flags().Lookup(name)
		assert.NotNil(t, f, "ddx work must have --%s passthrough flag", name)
	}
	assert.NotNil(t, workCmd.Flags().Lookup("ignore-cooldown"), "ddx work must expose --ignore-cooldown")
	assert.NotNil(t, workCmd.Flags().Lookup("reason"), "ddx work must expose --reason")
	assert.Equal(t, "10", workCmd.Flags().Lookup("claim-rate-window").DefValue, "ddx work must default claim-rate window to 10")
	assert.Equal(t, "0", workCmd.Flags().Lookup("claim-rate-threshold").DefValue, "ddx work must default claim-rate threshold to 0.0")
	assert.Equal(t, "5", workCmd.Flags().Lookup("preclaim-warn-threshold").DefValue, "ddx work must default preclaim warn threshold to 5")
}

func TestWorkCommandSuppressesUsageAndRawErrorOnInterrupt(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()

	workCmd, _, err := root.Find([]string{"work"})
	require.NoError(t, err, "ddx work must exist")
	require.NotNil(t, workCmd)

	assert.True(t, workCmd.SilenceUsage, "interrupted work must not print usage")
	assert.True(t, workCmd.SilenceErrors, "interrupted work must not print a duplicate raw error")
}

func TestWork_CtrlC_ExitsCleanlyNoUsageNoRawError(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
	env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
	env.CreateDefaultConfig()
	store := bead.NewStore(env.Dir + "/.ddx")
	require.NoError(t, store.Init(context.Background()))
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), &bead.Bead{
		ID:    "work-interrupt-001",
		Title: "Interrupted work bead",
	}))

	factory := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir)
	runner := &blockingTryExecutor{started: make(chan struct{})}
	factory.AgentRunnerOverride = &tryHookRunnerStub{t: t}
	factory.tryExecutorOverride = runner
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()

	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	root.SetContext(ctx)

	var buf bytes.Buffer
	root.SetOut(&buf)
	root.SetErr(&buf)
	root.SetArgs([]string{"work", "--once", "--no-review", "--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing"})

	done := make(chan error, 1)
	go func() {
		done <- root.Execute()
	}()

	select {
	case <-runner.started:
	case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
		t.Fatal("work executor did not start")
	}
	cancel()

	var err error
	select {
	case err = <-done:
	case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
		t.Fatal("work command did not return after cancel")
	}

	var exitErr *ExitError
	require.ErrorAs(t, err, &exitErr)
	assert.Equal(t, 130, exitErr.Code)
	assert.Empty(t, exitErr.Message)
	assert.Empty(t, err.Error())

	out := buf.String()
	assert.Contains(t, out, "worker exited:")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "failed:")
	assert.NotContains(t, out, "Usage:")
	assert.NotContains(t, out, "Error: context canceled")
	assert.False(t, errors.Is(err, context.Canceled), "work should convert plain interrupt to exit 130 after writing its summary")
}

     7	// cmdSetProcessGroup is a no-op on Windows; process group semantics differ.
     8	func cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd *exec.Cmd) {}
     9	
    10	// cmdKillProcessGroup falls back to killing only the leader process on Windows.
    11	func cmdKillProcessGroup(cmd *exec.Cmd) {
    12		if cmd.Process != nil {

     1	//go:build !linux && !windows
     2	
     3	package agent
     4	
     5	import (
     6		"os/exec"
     7		"runtime"
     8		"testing"
     9	)
    10	
    11	// TestOSExecutorExecuteInDir_NonLinuxParentDeathFallbackDocumentsGap documents
    12	// the parent-death cleanup gap on non-Linux platforms. executor_unix.go sets
    13	// Pdeathsig in SysProcAttr for all non-Windows platforms, but the kernel only
    14	// enforces it on Linux (and NetBSD). On macOS and other BSDs the field compiles
    15	// and is accepted but has no effect.
    16	//
    17	// Compensating controls on non-Linux platforms:
    18	//   - cmdKillProcessGroup via ctx cancellation (graceful-shutdown path in executor.go)
    19	//   - startup orphan reaper (ddx-8f2e0ebf) as a final backstop for already-dead workers
    20	//
    21	// This test verifies that process-group isolation (Setpgid) is still configured
    22	// so that cmdKillProcessGroup can reach all descendants via kill(-pgid), and
    23	// logs the platform gap so reviewers have an explicit record.
    24	func TestOSExecutorExecuteInDir_NonLinuxParentDeathFallbackDocumentsGap(t *testing.T) {
    25		cmd := exec.Command("sleep", "1")
    26		cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd)
    27	
    28		if cmd.SysProcAttr == nil {
    29			t.Fatal("SysProcAttr must be set: process-group isolation is required even on non-Linux platforms")
    30		}
    31		if !cmd.SysProcAttr.Setpgid {
    32			t.Error("Setpgid must be true so cmdKillProcessGroup can reach all descendants via kill(-pgid)")
    33		}
    34	
    35		// Pdeathsig is set in SysProcAttr (executor_unix.go) but is not kernel-enforced
    36		// on this platform. Abnormal-exit orphan cleanup relies on the compensating
    37		// controls documented above.
    38		t.Logf("non-Linux platform (%s): Pdeathsig in SysProcAttr is not kernel-enforced; "+
    39			"graceful-cancel path (cmdKillProcessGroup) and orphan-reaper (ddx-8f2e0ebf) "+
    40			"are the compensating controls", runtime.GOOS)
    41	}

     1	//go:build !windows
     2	
     3	package agent
     4	
     5	import (
     6		"context"
     7		"os"
     8		"os/exec"
     9		"strings"

    11		"syscall"
    12		"testing"
    13		"time"
    14	)
    15	
    16	// TestCmdSetProcessGroup_Pdeathsig verifies that cmdSetProcessGroup sets
    17	// Pdeathsig to SIGKILL on Linux to ensure harness children die when the
    18	// parent worker dies abnormally.
    19	func TestCmdSetProcessGroup_Pdeathsig(t *testing.T) {
    20		cmd := exec.Command("sleep", "60")
    21		cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd)
    22	
    23		if cmd.SysProcAttr == nil {
    24			t.Fatal("SysProcAttr is nil")
    25		}
    26		if !cmd.SysProcAttr.Setpgid {
    27			t.Error("Setpgid is not true")
    28		}
    29		if cmd.SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig != syscall.SIGKILL {
    30			t.Errorf("Pdeathsig = %v, want SIGKILL", cmd.SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig)
    31		}
    32	}
    33	
    34	// TestExecutor_OrphanKilledOnParentSIGKILL verifies that a harness child
    35	// does not survive when its parent worker is killed with SIGKILL.
    36	// This test spawns a test harness that forks a long-lived grandchild,
    37	// then kills the parent worker process and verifies both processes are gone.
    38	func TestExecutor_OrphanKilledOnParentSIGKILL(t *testing.T) {
    39		if testing.Short() {
    40			t.Skip("skipping in short mode")
    41		}
    42	
    43		// Create a simple test harness script that forks a long-lived process.
    44		// The child (harness) will itself spawn a grandchild to ensure the
    45		// process group is properly set.
    46		script := `#!/bin/bash
    47	# Start a long-lived process in the background
    48	sleep 300 &
    49	
    50	# Keep the harness running too
    51	sleep 300
    52	`
    53	
    54		tmpdir := t.TempDir()
    55		scriptPath := tmpdir + "/test-harness.sh"
    56		if err := os.WriteFile(scriptPath, []byte(script), 0755); err != nil {
    57			t.Fatalf("failed to write test harness: %v", err)
    58		}
    59	
    60		// We'll run this in a goroutine and kill the parent from outside
    61		var cmd *exec.Cmd
    62		var cmdMu sync.Mutex
    63		errCh := make(chan error, 1)
    64	
    65		go func() {
    66			// We simulate executing the harness via the executor.
    67			// But we need direct access to the cmd object to kill the parent.
    68			cmdMu.Lock()
    69			cmd = exec.Command(scriptPath)
    70			cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd)
    71			cmdMu.Unlock()
    72	
    73			// Start the command manually (not through executor.ExecuteInDir)
    74			// so we can kill the parent process from this test.
    75			if err := cmd.Start(); err != nil {
    76				errCh <- err
    77				return
    78			}
    79	
    80			// Wait for the process to complete (it will be killed)
    81			_ = cmd.Wait()
    82			errCh <- nil
    83		}()
    84	
    85		// Give the child time to spawn and the grandchild to start
    86		time.Sleep(500 * time.Millisecond)
    87	
    88		// Get the harness child PID
    89		cmdMu.Lock()
    90		if cmd == nil || cmd.Process == nil {
    91			cmdMu.Unlock()
    92			t.Fatal("child process not started")
    93		}
    94		childPID := cmd.Process.Pid
    95		cmdMu.Unlock()
    96	
    97		// Find the process group
    98		pgid, err := syscall.Getpgid(childPID)
    99		if err != nil {
   100			t.Errorf("failed to get pgid of child: %v", err)
   101		}
   102	
   103		// Check that the child is running
   104		childProc, err := os.FindProcess(childPID)
   105		if err != nil {
   106			t.Fatalf("child process not found: %v", err)
   107		}
   108	
   109		// Send signal 0 to verify the process is alive
   110		if err := childProc.Signal(syscall.Signal(0)); err != nil {
   111			t.Fatalf("child process is not running: %v", err)
   112		}
   113	
   114		// Now kill the process group (simulating what would happen when parent dies)
   115		_ = syscall.Kill(-pgid, syscall.SIGKILL)
   116	
   117		// Wait a bit for the process to be reaped
   118		time.Sleep(500 * time.Millisecond)
   119	
   120		// Verify the child is gone
   121		if _, err := os.FindProcess(childPID); err == nil {
   122			// Process still exists; try signal 0 again
   123			childProc2, _ := os.FindProcess(childPID)
   124			if err := childProc2.Signal(syscall.Signal(0)); err == nil {
   125				t.Errorf("child process %d is still running after parent death", childPID)
   126			}
   127		}
   128	
   129		// Wait for the goroutine to complete
   130		<-errCh
   131	}
   132	
   133	// TestExecutor_GracefulShutdownKillsChild verifies that graceful shutdown
   134	// (SIGTERM/SIGINT) still properly terminates child processes.
   135	func TestExecutor_GracefulShutdownKillsChild(t *testing.T) {
   136		if testing.Short() {
   137			t.Skip("skipping in short mode")
   138		}
   139	
   140		executor := &OSExecutor{}
   141	
   142		// Create a context that we'll cancel to simulate graceful shutdown
   143		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
   144	
   145		// Cancel the context to trigger graceful shutdown
   146		time.AfterFunc(100*time.Millisecond, cancel)
   147	
   148		// Start a long-running process

   150		result, err := executor.ExecuteInDir(ctx, "sh", []string{"-c", "sleep 300"}, "", tempdir)
   151

   166	func TestExecutor_DoesNotMigrateThreadDuringStart(t *testing.T) {
   167		// This test is a sanity check: verify that we can spawn a process and it

   170		// The code review and deployment will verify the thread-locking behavior.
   171	
   172		executor := &OSExecutor{}

   175	
   176		// Execute a simple command that completes quickly
   177		result, err := executor.ExecuteInDir(ctx, "echo", []string{"test"}, "", tempdir)
   178		if err != nil {
   179			t.Fatalf("execution failed: %v", err)
   180		}
   181	
   182		if result.ExitCode != 0 {

    36	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
    37	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, powerClass bounds, etc.) live on
    38	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
    39	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
    40		Log             io.Writer

    48		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
    49		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
    50		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
    51		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
    52		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
    53		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
    54		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
    55		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
    56		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
    57		// ProseEvidenceHook runs after a successful (landed) attempt and before
    58		// CloseWithEvidence so docs-changing attempts attach advisory prose-check
    59		// evidence to the bead's event history. Errors are logged and ignored;
    60		// prose findings never block closure.
    61		ProseEvidenceHook ProseEvidenceHook
    62		// PostAttemptDecompositionHook is called when a no_changes attempt signals
    63		// orchestrator_action: decompose in its rationale, indicating the bead is too
    64		// large for implementation-level splitting. The hook should run the same
    65		// orchestrator splitter used by the intake gate and return child specs and an
    66		// AC map. A nil return or error falls back to operator-required parking.
    67		PostAttemptDecompositionHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (*PreClaimDecomposition, error)
    68		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
    69		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
    70		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
    71		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
    72		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
    73		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
    74		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
    75		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
    76		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
    77		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
    78		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
    79		// PreClaimTimeout bounds the pre-claim readiness hooks. Zero means use the
    80		// binary default so a hanging readiness check cannot park the worker
    81		// forever.
    82		PreClaimTimeout time.Duration
    83		// PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold is the number of consecutive pre-claim warn
    84		// fingerprints across distinct bead IDs that trips the operator-attention
    85		// guard. Zero means use DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold.
    86		PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold int
    87		// RouteResolutionTimeout bounds the routing preflight (RoutePreflight) and
    88		// the resolveRoute viability check so a hung resolver cannot wedge the
    89		// single-threaded worker for hours while a lease is held (ddx-8f2e0ebf).
    90		// Zero means use DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout (60s).
    91		RouteResolutionTimeout time.Duration
    92		// ConsecutiveWedgeThreshold is the number of consecutive wedges
    93		// (route_resolution_timeout / progress_watchdog) on the same bead that
    94		// trips the consecutive-wedge guard: the worker stops re-claiming the bead
    95		// and parks it for operator attention. Zero means use
    96		// DefaultConsecutiveWedgeThreshold (2) (ddx-9714eaac).
    97		ConsecutiveWedgeThreshold int
    98		// ClaimSuccessRateWindow and ClaimSuccessRateThreshold control the rolling
    99		// claim-throughput warning emitted by ddx work. A full window whose claim
   100		// success rate is at or below the configured threshold triggers a single
   101		// operator-visible warning until a later successful claim clears the warned
   102		// state.
   103		ClaimSuccessRateWindow    int
   104		ClaimSuccessRateThreshold float64
   105		// BudgetStop, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next bead. It
   106		// lets CLI/server callers surface already-tripped budget state as a typed
   107		// work-layer StopCondition before any new bead is claimed. The companion
   108		// report is still appended for operator visibility, but the stop itself is
   109		// classified through work.StopConditionBudget.
   110		BudgetStop func() (work.StopDecision, ExecuteBeadReport, bool)
   111		// BinaryRefreshCheck, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next
   112		// bead. A true result means the caller has started a replacement worker
   113		// with equivalent arguments and this loop should exit before claiming work.
   114		// Errors are logged and fail open so update-probing never blocks the queue.
   115		BinaryRefreshCheck func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error)
   116		// ProjectRootDirtyCheck, when non-nil, is called at the top of each loop
   117		// iteration before any bead is claimed. A non-empty return value means the
   118		// canonical project root has uncommitted tracked non-.ddx changes. The
   119		// worker emits loop.operator_attention and stops instead of proceeding to
   120		// claim. Repeated exits for the same dirty path-set are tracked durably so

  7752	// sleepOrWake sleeps for d unless ctx is cancelled or a wake signal arrives on
  7753	// wakeCh. A wake signal returns nil (the loop re-scans the queue) so callers
  7754	// like operatorPromptApprove can shorten the next-tick latency from a full
  7755	// PollInterval to ~0. wakeCh may be nil — in which case this is equivalent to
  7756	// sleepWithContext.
  7757	func sleepOrWake(ctx context.Context, d time.Duration, wakeCh <-chan struct{}) error {
  7758		if d <= 0 {
  7759			return nil
  7760		}
  7761		if wakeCh == nil {
  7762			return sleepWithContext(ctx, d)
  7763		}
  7764		timer := time.NewTimer(d)
  7765		defer timer.Stop()
  7766		select {
  7767		case <-ctx.Done():
  7768			return ctx.Err()
  7769		case <-timer.C:
  7770			return nil
  7771		case <-wakeCh:
  7772			return nil
  7773		}

  1581		// still subsystem-specific exits.
  1582		exitReason := ""

  1589		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{

  1610		releaser, _ := w.Store.(orphanHarnessLeaseReleaser)

  1612		if reaped, reapErr := reapOrphanedHarnessChildren(

  1617			releaser,

  1619			assignee,

  1621			emit,

  1623		); reapErr != nil {

  1626			}
  1627			emit("loop.operator_attention", map[string]any{
  1628				"reason":       "orphan_harness_reaper_failed",
  1629				"project_root": runtime.ProjectRoot,

  1631			})
  1632		} else if reaped > 0 && runtime.Log != nil {

  1634		}

  1640			if timedOut {

  1642				// is nothing to release. Log, emit a non-fatal warning, and continue
  1643				// into the drain loop — the next claim iteration calls RoutePreflight

  1649				}

  1678				emitDisruptionDetected(emit, w.Store, "", "preflight_rejected", detail, harness, model, assignee, now().UTC())

  1686		defer func() {

  1724			sawParentBackEdge:          sawParentBackEdge,

  1736		for {

data.text: I’ve got the injection story. The last piece is how reports become transport outcomes, because the current classifier short-circuits once `OutcomeReason` is set. I’m reading that path before I change anything so the new signal wording doesn’t get swallowed.

   117	func fromTryRateLimitRetryInfo(info agenttry.RateLimitRetryInfo) RateLimitRetryInfo {

     1	package agent
     2	
     3	import "strings"
     4	
     5	// OperatorCancelReason marks a landing preserved because the operator
     6	// cancelled the attempt mid-flight via /api/beads/<id>/cancel. ADR-022
     7	// §Cancel SLA: the worker aborts at the next safe point (between LLM turns /
     8	// git ops) and emits a preserved_for_review result with this exact reason.
     9	const OperatorCancelReason = "operator_cancel"
    10	
    11	// PushFailedReasonPrefix is the canonical Reason prefix written by the
    12	// landing layer when `git push` rejects the new tip after a local merge or
    13	// fast-forward succeeded. ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus matches on this prefix
    14	// to map a merged-locally-but-not-pushed outcome to push_failed instead of
    15	// success, so the bead stays open.
    16	const PushFailedReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push failed:"
    17	
    18	// PushConflictReasonPrefix marks a more specific failure mode than
    19	// PushFailedReasonPrefix: the local push was rejected because the remote
    20	// advanced, the loop attempted an automatic pull/merge to recover, and the
    21	// merge produced a conflict the loop cannot resolve. The bead is parked for
    22	// human review with a short cooldown rather than a year-long park.
    23	const PushConflictReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push conflict:"
    24	
    25	// FailureMode constants classify *why* an execution did not land cleanly.
    26	// They are orthogonal to Outcome/Status: a single failed attempt carries one
    27	// outcome (e.g. preserved) and one failure mode (e.g. merge_conflict) so
    28	// measurement surfaces can aggregate failures by cause.
    29	const (
    30		FailureModeContextOverflow                = "context_overflow"
    31		FailureModeMergeConflict                  = "merge_conflict"
    32		FailureModeTestFailure                    = "test_failure"
    33		FailureModeBuildFailure                   = "build_failure"
    34		FailureModeTimeout                        = "timeout"
    35		FailureModeAuthError                      = "auth_error"
    36		FailureModeNoChanges                      = "no_changes"
    37		FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced             = "no_evidence_produced"
    38		FailureModeRatchetMiss                    = "ratchet_miss"
    39		FailureModeNoViableProvider               = "no_viable_provider"
    40		FailureModeProviderConnectivity           = "provider_connectivity"
    41		FailureModeServerUnavailable              = "server_unavailable"
    42		FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled            = "harness_not_installed"
    43		FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint = "blocked_by_passthrough_constraint"
    44		FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied          = "agent_power_unsatisfied"
    45		FailureModeLockContention                 = "lock_contention"
    46		FailureModeWorktreeLost                   = "worktree_lost"
    47		// FailureModeRouteResolutionTimeout classifies an attempt that was parked
    48		// because route resolution / routing preflight exceeded the configured
    49		// route-resolution timeout. The lease is released and the bead is flagged
    50		// for operator attention rather than auto-retried (ddx-d8970a7b).
    51		FailureModeRouteResolutionTimeout = "route_resolution_timeout"
    52		// FailureModeProgressWatchdog classifies an attempt the progress watchdog
    53		// terminated because phase-empty heartbeats (harness/model/route all empty)
    54		// persisted past the phase budget. The worker appeared fresh to the
    55		// liveness TTL while making no forward progress, so the lease is released
    56		// and the bead is flagged for operator attention (ddx-dc23f001, parent
    57		// ddx-8f2e0ebf criterion B).
    58		FailureModeProgressWatchdog = "progress_watchdog"
    59		// FailureModeConsecutiveWedge classifies a bead the consecutive-wedge guard
    60		// stopped re-claiming because it wedged (route_resolution_timeout or
    61		// progress_watchdog) on consecutive claims up to the threshold. The bead is
    62		// parked to proposed for operator attention so the single-threaded worker
    63		// keeps draining the rest of the queue instead of re-wedging the same bead
    64		// indefinitely (ddx-9714eaac, parent ddx-8f2e0ebf criterion E).
    65		FailureModeConsecutiveWedge = "consecutive_wedge"
    66		// FailureModeAttemptIntegrity classifies an attempt that produced commits
    67		// and a clean agent exit but failed DDx's structured post-agent integrity
    68		// validation (post-commit mutation, empty pre-commit gate evidence, or
    69		// uncommitted tracked changes after the implementation commit). It is
    70		// distinct from implementation/agent failures so operators can tell a DDx
    71		// validation rejection apart from a genuine task failure (ddx-725b65b4).
    72		FailureModeAttemptIntegrity = "attempt_integrity"
    73		// FailureModeLandRetry classifies land/ref/index coordination failures
    74		// where the implementation result is still valid and the worker should
    75		// retry/reconcile the landing step instead of escalating implementation.
    76		FailureModeLandRetry = "land_retry"
    77		// FailureModeLandOperatorAttention classifies land/ref/index coordination
    78		// failures where local staged state includes operator-owned work and must
    79		// be reviewed before DDx lands or retries the result.
    80		FailureModeLandOperatorAttention = "land_operator_attention"
    81		FailureModeUnknown               = "unknown"
    82	)
    83	
    84	// ResourceExhaustedStopMessage is the operator-visible message emitted when
    85	// execution must stop because the host cannot safely continue draining the
    86	// queue.
    87	const ResourceExhaustedStopMessage = "resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop"
    88	
    89	// ClassifyFailureMode derives a failure_mode for a worker-level result from
    90	// its outcome, exit code, and error message. Returns "" when the outcome is
    91	// task_succeeded (success carries no failure mode). task_no_changes always
    92	// maps to FailureModeNoChanges regardless of error text.
    93	//
    94	// For task_failed (or non-zero exit code), the error message is scanned for
    95	// well-known substrings. The order matters: context/auth/timeout patterns
    96	// are checked before generic test/build patterns so a "test timed out"
    97	// classifies as timeout rather than test_failure. Anything unrecognised
    98	// maps to FailureModeUnknown so aggregates never hide a missed pattern.
    99	func ClassifyFailureMode(outcome string, exitCode int, errMsg string) string {
   100		switch outcome {
   101		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
   102			if exitCode == 0 {
   103				return ""
   104			}
   105			// Degenerate: success outcome with non-zero exit. Treat as unknown
   106			// failure rather than success so the aggregate flags the anomaly.
   107		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
   108			return FailureModeNoChanges
   109		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
   110			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
   111		}
   112	
   113		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
   114		switch {
   115		case IsLockContentionError(errMsg):
   116			return FailureModeLockContention
   117		case IsWorktreeLostError(errMsg):
   118			return FailureModeWorktreeLost
   119		case containsAny(lower,
   120			"passthrough constraint unsatisfiable",
   121			"passthrough constraint:",
   122			"max_power is less than min_power",
   123			"max_power exceeds min_power",
   124			"must be less than max_power",
   125			"no viable routing candidate for pins",
   126			"harness cannot satisfy power constraint",
   127			"harness pin incompatible with power bounds",
   128			"model pin incompatible with power bounds",
   129			"provider pin incompatible with power bounds"):
   130			return FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint
   131		case containsAny(lower,
   132			"agent power unsatisfied",
   133			"no model meets min_power",
   134			"no model with power >=",
   135			"minimum power not achievable",
   136			"min_power constraint cannot be satisfied"):
   137			return FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied
   138		case containsAny(lower,
   139			"executable file not found in $path",
   140			"executable file not found",
   141			"no such file or directory") &&
   142			containsAny(lower, "exec:", "harness", "claude", "agent", "codex", "gemini"):
   143			return FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled
   144		case containsAny(lower,
   145			"no viable harness",
   146			"no harness configured",
   147			"failed to initialize routing service",
   148			"resolveroute:",
   149			"no viable routing candidate",
   150			"no live provider supports",
   151			"no candidate satisfying local endpoint",
   152			"no viable provider"):
   153			return FailureModeNoViableProvider
   154		case isServerTransportFailureText(lower):
   155			return FailureModeServerUnavailable
   156		case containsAny(lower,
   157			"context length",
   158			"context_length_exceeded",
   159			"context window",
   160			"maximum context",
   161			"prompt is too long",
   162			"prompt too long",
   163			"token limit",
   164			"too many tokens",
   165			"exceeds the context"):
   166			return FailureModeContextOverflow
   167		case containsAny(lower,
   168			"unauthorized",
   169			"401 unauthorized",
   170			"invalid api key",
   171			"invalid_api_key",
   172			"authentication failed",
   173			"authentication_error",
   174			"no api key",
   175			"missing api key",
   176			"quota exceeded",
   177			"insufficient quota",
   178			"insufficient_quota",
   179			"rate limit",
   180			"rate_limit",
   181			"429"):
   182			return FailureModeAuthError
   183		case containsAny(lower,
   184			"timeout",
   185			"timed out",
   186			"context deadline exceeded",
   187			"deadline exceeded",
   188			"signal: killed"):
   189			return FailureModeTimeout
   190		case containsAny(lower,
   191			"merge conflict",
   192			"ff-merge not possible",
   193			"merge failed",
   194			"automatic merge failed"):
   195			return FailureModeMergeConflict
   196		case containsAny(lower,
   197			"build failed",
   198			"compilation failed",
   199			"compile error",
   200			"compilation error",
   201			"cannot find package",
   202			"undefined:",
   203			"undefined reference",
   204			"syntax error"):
   205			return FailureModeBuildFailure
   206		case containsAny(lower,
   207			"test failed",
   208			"tests failed",
   209			"--- fail:",
   210			"assertion failed",
   211			"test failure"):
   212			return FailureModeTestFailure
   213		}
   214		if outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed && errMsg != "" {
   215			return FailureModeUnknown
   216		}
   217		if exitCode != 0 {
   218			return FailureModeUnknown
   219		}
   220		return ""

   202	// AttemptDiagnostic captures infrastructure state when an attempt cannot be
   203	// inspected normally. It is intentionally small and JSON-native so result.json
   204	// remains useful even when the worker worktree has already vanished.

   217	// BeadEventAppender records append-only evidence events on a bead.
   218	// Implemented by *bead.Store — kept as a minimal interface so the agent
   219	// package does not need to import a concrete store type in tests.
   220	type BeadEventAppender interface {
   221		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
   222	}
   223	
   224	func appendWorkEvent(store BeadEventAppender, beadID, kind, summary string, body map[string]any, actor string, at time.Time) {
   225		if store == nil || beadID == "" {
   226			return
   227		}
   228		encodedBody, err := json.Marshal(body)
   229		if err != nil {
   230			return
   231		}

   233			Kind:      kind,
   234			Summary:   summary,
   235			Body:      string(encodedBody),
   236			Actor:     actor,
   237			Source:    "ddx work",
   238			CreatedAt: at,
   239		})
   240	}

  1455	
  1456		// Operator-cancel override (ADR-022 §Cancel SLA). When the mid-attempt
  1457		// poll detected cancel-requested:true on the bead, force the result into
  1458		// the preserved_for_review channel with reason "operator_cancel" so the
  1459		// orchestrator and supervisor surface it as an operator-driven preserve
  1460		// rather than a generic task_failed/no_evidence outcome.
  1461		if cancelHonored != nil && cancelHonored.Load() {
  1462			res.Outcome = "preserved"
  1463			res.Reason = OperatorCancelReason
  1464			res.Error = ""
  1465			res.ExitCode = 0
  1466			res.FailureMode = ""
  1467			res.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
  1468			res.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, OperatorCancelReason, "")
  1469			_ = writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, res)
  1470			if err := publishAttemptResult(res); err != nil {
  1471				return res, fmt.Errorf("publishing attempt result: %w", err)
  1472			}
  1473			return res, nil
  1474		}
  1475	
  1476		// Classify failure mode from worker-level signals. ApplyLandingToResult
  1477		// may refine this with landing-level signals (merge conflict, gate
  1478		// failure) before the final result is output.
  1479		res.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)
  1480	
  1481		// Structured post-agent integrity validation (ddx-725b65b4). A clean agent
  1482		// exit with a commit is not sufficient evidence of a contract-honoring
  1483		// attempt: reject attempts that rewrote the implementation commit after the
  1484		// first commit, left required pre-commit gate evidence empty, or left tracked
  1485		// files uncommitted. Such attempts are demoted to task_failed with a distinct
  1486		// FailureMode so the orchestrator preserves them for review (rather than
  1487		// merging) and an operator can tell the DDx validation rejection apart from
  1488		// an implementation failure. The evidence-bundle commit (below) has not run
  1489		// yet, so the reflog only carries the agent's own commits.
  1490		if res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded && res.ExitCode == 0 && res.ImplementationRev != "" {
  1491			verdict := ValidateAttemptIntegrity(AttemptIntegrityInput{
  1492				BaseRev:           baseRev,
  1493				ImplementationRev: res.ImplementationRev,
  1494				CommitEvents:      readWorktreeCommitEvents(wtPath),
  1495				DirtyPaths:        integrityDirtyPaths(wtPath),
  1496				CodeChanging:      true,
  1497			})
  1498			if !verdict.OK {
  1499				res.Outcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed
  1500				res.Reason = AttemptIntegrityPreserveReason
  1501				res.Error = verdict.Detail
  1502				res.FailureMode = FailureModeAttemptIntegrity
  1503			}
  1504		}
  1505	
  1506		if err := publishAttemptResult(res); err != nil {
  1507			return res, fmt.Errorf("publishing attempt result: %w", err)
  1508		}

  1512		// can separate intent from resolved outcome (AC5 of ddx-20047dd5).
  1513		appendBeadRoutingEvidence(runtime.BeadEvents, beadID, resultHarness, resultProvider, resultModel, routeReason, routeBaseURL, rcfg.Passthrough(), rcfg.MinPower(), rcfg.MaxPower(), actualPower)

223	// ReportFromExecuteBeadResult maps the detailed worker/orchestrator result
   224	// into the loop-facing report shape used by ddx work and ddx try.
   225	func ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res *ExecuteBeadResult, powerClass string) ExecuteBeadReport {
   226		if res == nil {
   227			return ExecuteBeadReport{}
   228		}
   229		return ExecuteBeadReport{
   230			BeadID:                      res.BeadID,
   231			AttemptID:                   res.AttemptID,
   232			WorkerID:                    res.WorkerID,
   233			Harness:                     res.Harness,
   234			Provider:                    res.Provider,
   235			Model:                       res.Model,
   236			ActualPower:                 res.ActualPower,
   237			PredictedPower:              res.PredictedPower,
   238			PredictedSpeedTPS:           res.PredictedSpeedTPS,
   239			PredictedCostUSDPer1kTokens: res.PredictedCostUSDPer1kTokens,
   240			PredictedCostSource:         res.PredictedCostSource,
   241			Status:                      res.Status,
   242			Detail:                      res.Detail,
   243			Error:                       res.Error,
   244			Stderr:                      res.Stderr,
   245			RateLimitBudget:             res.RateLimitBudget,
   246			SessionID:                   res.SessionID,
   247			BaseRev:                     res.BaseRev,
   248			ResultRev:                   res.ResultRev,
   249			ImplementationRev:           res.ImplementationRev,
   250			LandedRev:                   res.LandedRev,
   251			TargetBranch:                res.TargetBranch,
   252			EvidenceRev:                 res.EvidenceRev,
   253			ProjectRoot:                 res.ProjectRoot,
   254			PreserveRef:                 res.PreserveRef,
   255			CandidateRef:                res.CandidateRef,
   256			CycleIndex:                  res.CycleIndex,
   257			NoChangesRationale:          res.NoChangesRationale,
   258			CycleTrace:                  append([]ExecutionCycleTrace(nil), res.CycleTrace...),
   259			PowerClass:                  powerClass,
   260			CostUSD:                     res.CostUSD,
   261			DurationMS:                  int64(res.DurationMS),
   262			ResourceExhausted:           res.ResourceExhausted,
   263			OutcomeReason:               res.FailureMode,
   264		}
   265	}

- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)
- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present

- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.

## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)

- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug
- GraphQL under the nested path
- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404
- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths
- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST

## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)

### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced

B1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.

Wire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.

Create the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.

The legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.

### 2. Audit attribution not populated

F1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.

Find every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(&request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.

### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database

`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: &str = "x-axon-database"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:

(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR
(b) A new field on every request proto

(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `"{tenant}:{database}"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.

Also add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.

For this bead, the gRPC change is small:
- Rename the metadata constant
- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form
- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form
- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`

### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)

D2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.

## Scope summary

This bead does exactly three things:

1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)
2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present
3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata

## Tests for this bead

### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)

- create a JwtIssuer with a known key
- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)
- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses
- send a request with Authorization: Bearer <jwt> to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections
- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)

### Integration test: attribution populated

- send an entity-create request with a JWT
- query the audit log
- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub

### gRPC test: composite slug

- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`
- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)

### Manual smoke test

Start the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.

## Out of scope

- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred
- OIDC federation — follow-up
- Signing key rotation — future ADR
- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths

## Files to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)
- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup
- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path
- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW
- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW
- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface

## Files NOT to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it
- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal
- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes
- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes

## Verification

JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\n+};\n+use axon_server::actor_scope::ActorScopeGuard;\n+use axon_server::auth::AuthContext;\n+use axon_server::auth_pipeline::JwtIssuer;\n+use axon_server::control_plane::ControlPlaneDb;\n+use axon_server::control_plane_routes::ControlPlaneState;\n+use axon_server::cors_config::CorsStore;\n+use axon_server::gateway::build_router_with_auth;\n+use axon_server::rate_limit::RateLimitConfig;\n+use axon_server::tenant_router::{TenantHandler, TenantRouter};\n+use axon_server::user_roles::UserRoleStore;\n+use axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\n+use axon_storage::memory::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n+\n+// ── Constants ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+\n+const ISSUER: &str = \"cutover-issuer\";\n+const TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\n+const DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\n+const COLLECTION: &str = \"items\";\n+const USER_ID: &str = \"u-cutover-01\";\n+const SECRET: &[u8] = b\"cutover-test-secret-32bytes-xxxxx\";\n+\n+fn now_secs() -> u64 {\n+    SystemTime::now()\n+        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n+        .unwrap_or_default()\n+        .as_secs()\n+}\n+\n+// ── Test fixtures ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+\n+/// Build the pre-seeded auth storage (user + tenant member).\n+fn make_auth_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n+    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n+    storage.insert_user(User {\n+        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n+        display_name: \"Cutover User\".to_string(),\n+        email: None,\n+        created_at_ms: 0,\n+        suspended_at_ms: None,\n+    });\n+    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n+        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n+        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n+        role: TenantRole::Write,\n+    });\n+    storage\n+}\n+\n+/// Build a `ControlPlaneState` with jwt_issuer + auth storage so the optional\n+/// JWT verification layer is enabled in the HTTP router.\n+fn make_control_plane(issuer: Arc<JwtIssuer>, auth_storage: MemoryStorageAdapter) -> ControlPlaneState {\n+    let db = ControlPlaneDb::open_in_memory().expect(\"in-memory control-plane db should open\");\n+    let db = Arc::new(TokioMutex::new(db));\n+    let boxed: Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> = Box::new(auth_storage);\n+    let storage_arc: Arc<Mutex<Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>>> =\n+        Arc::new(Mutex::new(boxed));\n+    ControlPlaneState::new(db, PathBuf::from(\".\"), UserRoleStore::default(), CorsStore::default())\n+        .with_jwt_issuer(issuer)\n+        .with_storage(storage_arc)\n+}\n+\n+/// Build an `axum_test::TestServer` backed by an in-memory data-plane handler.\n+///\n+/// Returns the test server and the data-plane handler so tests can inspect the\n+/// audit log directly.\n+fn make_server(\n+    issuer: Arc<JwtIssuer>,\n+) -> (axum_test::TestServer, TenantHandler) {\n+    let auth_storage = make_auth_storage();\n+    let control_plane = make_control_plane(issuer, auth_storage);\n+\n+    let data_storage: Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n+        Box::new(MemoryStorageAdapter::default());\n+    let handler: TenantHandler =\n+        Arc::new(TokioMutex::new(AxonHandler::new(data_storage)));\n+    let tenant_router = Arc::new(TenantRouter::single(handler.clone()));\n+\n+    let app = build_router_with_auth(\n+        tenant_router,\n+        \"memory\",\n+        None,\n+        AuthContext::no_auth(),\n+        RateLimitConfig::default(),\n+        ActorScopeGuard::default(),\n+        Some(control_plane),\n+        CorsStore::default(),\n+    );\n+\n+    (axum_test::TestServer::new(app), handler)\n+}\n+\n+fn issuer() -> Arc<JwtIssuer> {\n+    Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string()))\n+}\n+\n+fn write_grants() -> Grants {\n+    Grants {\n+        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n+            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n+            ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n+        }],\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+fn read_only_grants() -> Grants {\n+    Grants {\n+        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n+            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n+            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n+        }],\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+fn make_token(issuer: &JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -> String {\n+    let now = now_secs();\n+    let claims = JwtClaims {\n+        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n+        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n+        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n+        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n+        iat: now,\n+        nbf: now,\n+        exp: now + 3600,\n+        grants,\n+    };\n+    issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"token issuance should succeed\")\n+}\n+\n+fn entity_path(id: &str) -> String {\n+    format!(\"/tenants/{TENANT}/databases/{DATABASE}/entities/{COLLECTION}/{id}\")\n+}\n+\n+fn entity_body(value: i32) -> Value {\n+    json!({ \"data\": { \"value\": value } })\n+}\n+\n+// ── Test 1: No auth header falls through to no-auth mode ─────────────────────\n+\n+#[tokio::test]\n+async fn cutover_no_auth_header_falls_through_to_no_auth_201() {\n+    let jw = issuer();\n+    let (server, _handler) = make_server(jw);\n+\n+    // No Authorization header → goes through no-auth path → 201\n+    let resp = server\n+        .post(&entity_path(\"e-noauth-01\"))\n+        .json(&entity_body(1))\n+        .await;\n+    resp.assert_status(axum::http::StatusCode::CREATED);\n+}\n+\n+// ── Test 2: Valid JWT with write grant → 201 ─────────────────────────────────\n+\n+#[tokio::test]\n+async fn cutover_valid_jwt_write_grant_creates_entity_201() {\n+    let jw = issuer();\n+    let token = make_token(&jw, write_grants());\n+    let (server, _handler) = make_server(jw);\n+\n+    let resp = server\n+        .post(&entity_path(\"e-jwt-write-01\"))\n+        .add_header(\n+            axum::http::header::AUTHORIZATION,\n+            axum::http::HeaderValue::from_str(&format!(\"Bearer {token}\")).unwrap(),\n+        )\n+        .json(&entity_body(42))\n+        .await;\n+    resp.assert_status(axum::http::StatusCode::CREATED);\n+    let body: Value = resp.json();\n+    assert_eq!(body[\"entity\"][\"id\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"), \"e-jwt-write-01\");\n+}\n+\n+// ── Test 3: JWT with read-only grant blocks POST → 403 ───────────────────────\n+\n+#[tokio::test]\n+async fn cutover_jwt_read_only_grant_blocks_post_403() {\n+    let jw = issuer();\n+    let token = make_token(&jw, read_only_grants());\n+    let (server, _handler) = make_server(jw);\n+\n+    let resp = server\n+        .post(&entity_path(\"e-jwt-read-01\"))\n+        .add_header(\n+            axum::http::header::AUTHORIZATION,\n+            axum::http::HeaderValue::from_str(&format!(\"Bearer {token}\")).unwrap(),\n+        )\n+        .json(&entity_body(1))\n+        .await;\n+    // JWT layer returns 403 because the write op is not in the read-only grants.\n+    resp.assert_status(axum::http::StatusCode::FORBIDDEN);\n+    let body: Value = resp.json();\n+    assert_eq!(\n+        body[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n+        \"op_not_granted\",\n+    );\n+}\n+\n+// ── Test 4: JWT write → attribution populated in audit entry ─────────────────\n+\n+#[tokio::test]\n+async fn cutover_audit_entry_has_attribution_after_jwt_write() {\n+    let jw = issuer();\n+    let token = make_token(&jw, write_grants());\n+    let (server, handler) = make_server(jw);\n+\n+    // Create the entity via JWT.\n+    let resp = server\n+        .post(&entity_path(\"e-attr-01\"))\n+        .add_header(\n+            axum::http::header::AUTHORIZATION,\n+            axum::http::HeaderValue::from_str(&format!(\"Bearer {token}\")).unwrap(),\n+        )\n+        .json(&entity_body(99))\n+        .await;\n+    resp.assert_status(axum::http::StatusCode::CREATED);\n+\n+    // Query the audit log directly from the data-plane handler.\n+    let audit_resp = handler\n+        .lock()\n+        .await\n+        .query_audit(QueryAuditRequest {\n+            database: None,\n+            collection: None,\n+            collection_ids: vec![],\n+            entity_id: None,\n+            actor: None,\n+            operation: Some(\"entity.create\".to_string()),\n+            since_ns: None,\n+            until_ns: None,\n+            after_id: None,\n+            limit: Some(10),\n+        })\n+        .expect(\"audit query should succeed\");\n+\n+    assert!(\n+        !audit_resp.entries.is_empty(),\n+        \"expected at least one audit entry after entity create\"\n+    );\n+\n+    let entry = audit_resp\n+        .entries\n+        .iter()\n+        .find(|e| e.entity_id.to_string() == \"e-attr-01\")\n+        .expect(\"entry for e-attr-01 must be present\");\n+\n+    let attribution = entry\n+        .attribution\n+        .as_ref()\n+        .expect(\"attribution must be set on JWT-authenticated write\");\n+\n+    assert_eq!(attribution.user_id, USER_ID, \"user_id must match JWT sub\");\n+    assert_eq!(attribution.tenant_id, TENANT, \"tenant_id must match JWT aud\");\n+    assert_eq!(attribution.auth_method, \"jwt\", \"auth_method must be 'jwt'\");\n+}\n+\n+// ── gRPC composite header parsing ────────────────────────────────────────────\n+\n+/// Verify that the composite `{tenant}:{database}` header value is parsed\n+/// correctly by `grpc_requested_database` (exercised indirectly here through\n+/// the handler calls in other tests, but we also do a direct unit-style check).\n+#[tokio::test]\n+async fn cutover_grpc_composite_header_roundtrip() {\n+    // This test verifies that a gRPC call using the new composite header format\n+    // succeeds in reaching the right database.  We do this by starting a\n+    // minimal gRPC service and issuing a create_entity call with the header.\n+    use axon_server::service::{AxonServiceImpl, AxonServiceServer};\n+    use axon_server::service::proto;\n+    use proto::axon_service_client::AxonServiceClient;\n+    use tonic::metadata::MetadataValue;\n+\n+    let svc = AxonServiceImpl::new_in_memory();\n+    let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(\"127.0.0.1:0\").await.unwrap();\n+    let addr = listener.local_addr().unwrap();\n+\n+    tokio::spawn(async move {\n+        tonic::transport::Server::builder()\n+            .add_service(AxonServiceServer::new(svc))\n+            .serve_with_incoming(tokio_stream::wrappers::TcpListenerStream::new(listener))\n+            .await\n+            .unwrap();\n+    });\n+\n+    tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n+\n+    let mut client = AxonServiceClient::connect(format!(\"http://{addr}\"))\n+        .await\n+        .unwrap();\n+\n+    // Construct a request with the composite `tenant:database` header.\n+    let mut req = tonic::Request::new(proto::CreateEntityRequest {\n+        collection: \"grpc-items\".into(),\n+        id: \"grpc-e-01\".into(),\n+        data_json: json!({\"x\": 1}).to_string(),\n+        actor: String::new(),\n+    });\n+    req.metadata_mut().insert(\n+        \"x-axon-tenant-database\",\n+        MetadataValue::try_from(\"acme:orders\").unwrap(),\n+    );\n+\n+    let resp = client.create_entity(req).await.expect(\"grpc create should succeed\");\n+    let entity = resp.into_inner().entity.unwrap();\n+    assert_eq!(entity.id, \"grpc-e-01\");\n+}","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0a9f1446-c2e0-4db8-b50e-318c50111461","uuid":"9151c21d-5adf-4d7d-a724-fbf7f18dd1b3","timestamp":"2026-04-16T01:10:02.067Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"diff     41	func (s *backEdgeDecompositionStore) Create(ctx context.Context, b *bead.Bead) error {
    42		base := s.Store
    43		if err := base.Create(ctx, b); err != nil {
    44			return err
    45		}
    46		if b.Parent != s.parentID {
    47			return nil
    48		}
    49		return base.Update(ctx, b.ID, func(child *bead.Bead) {
    50			child.AddDep(s.parentID, "blocks")
    51		})
    52	}

89	// ClassifyFailureMode derives a failure_mode for a worker-level result from
    90	// its outcome, exit code, and error message. Returns "" when the outcome is
    91	// task_succeeded (success carries no failure mode). task_no_changes always
    92	// maps to FailureModeNoChanges regardless of error text.
    93	//
    94	// For task_failed (or non-zero exit code), the error message is scanned for
    95	// well-known substrings. The order matters: context/auth/timeout patterns
    96	// are checked before generic test/build patterns so a "test timed out"
    97	// classifies as timeout rather than test_failure. Anything unrecognised
    98	// maps to FailureModeUnknown so aggregates never hide a missed pattern.
    99	func ClassifyFailureMode(outcome string, exitCode int, errMsg string) string {
   100		switch outcome {
   101		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
   102			if exitCode == 0 {
   103				return ""
   104			}
   105			// Degenerate: success outcome with non-zero exit. Treat as unknown
   106			// failure rather than success so the aggregate flags the anomaly.
   107		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
   108			return FailureModeNoChanges
   109		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
   110			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
   111		}
   112	
   113		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
   114		switch {
   115		case IsLockContentionError(errMsg):
   116			return FailureModeLockContention
   117		case IsWorktreeLostError(errMsg):
   118			return FailureModeWorktreeLost
   119		case containsAny(lower,
   120			"passthrough constraint unsatisfiable",
   121			"passthrough constraint:",
   122			"max_power is less than min_power",
   123			"max_power exceeds min_power",
   124			"must be less than max_power",
   125			"no viable routing candidate for pins",
   126			"harness cannot satisfy power constraint",
   127			"harness pin incompatible with power bounds",
   128			"model pin incompatible with power bounds",
   129			"provider pin incompatible with power bounds"):
   130			return FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint
   131		case containsAny(lower,
   132			"agent power unsatisfied",
   133			"no model meets min_power",
   134			"no model with power >=",
   135			"minimum power not achievable",
   136			"min_power constraint cannot be satisfied"):
   137			return FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied
   138		case containsAny(lower,
   139			"executable file not found in $path",
   140			"executable file not found",
   141			"no such file or directory") &&
   142			containsAny(lower, "exec:", "harness", "claude", "agent", "codex", "gemini"):
   143			return FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled
   144		case containsAny(lower,
   145			"no viable harness",
   146			"no harness configured",
   147			"failed to initialize routing service",
   148			"resolveroute:",
   149			"no viable routing candidate",
   150			"no live provider supports",
   151			"no candidate satisfying local endpoint",
   152			"no viable provider"):
   153			return FailureModeNoViableProvider
   154		case isServerTransportFailureText(lower):
   155			return FailureModeServerUnavailable
   156		case containsAny(lower,
   157			"context length",
   158			"context_length_exceeded",
   159			"context window",
   160			"maximum context",
   161			"prompt is too long",
   162			"prompt too long",
   163			"token limit",
   164			"too many tokens",
   165			"exceeds the context"):
   166			return FailureModeContextOverflow
   167		case containsAny(lower,
   168			"unauthorized",
   169			"401 unauthorized",
   170			"invalid api key",
   171			"invalid_api_key",
   172			"authentication failed",
   173			"authentication_error",
   174			"no api key",
   175			"missing api key",
   176			"quota exceeded",
   177			"insufficient quota",
   178			"insufficient_quota",
   179			"rate limit",
   180			"rate_limit",
   181			"429"):
   182			return FailureModeAuthError
   183		case containsAny(lower,
   184			"timeout",
   185			"timed out",
   186			"context deadline exceeded",
   187			"deadline exceeded",
   188			"signal: killed"):
   189			return FailureModeTimeout
   190		case containsAny(lower,
   191			"merge conflict",
   192			"ff-merge not possible",
   193			"merge failed",
   194			"automatic merge failed"):
   195			return FailureModeMergeConflict
   196		case containsAny(lower,
   197			"build failed",
   198			"compilation failed",
   199			"compile error",
   200			"compilation error",
   201			"cannot find package",
   202			"undefined:",
   203			"undefined reference",
   204			"syntax error"):
   205			return FailureModeBuildFailure
   206		case containsAny(lower,
   207			"test failed",
   208			"tests failed",
   209			"--- fail:",
   210			"assertion failed",
   211			"test failure"):
   212			return FailureModeTestFailure
   213		}
   214		if outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed && errMsg != "" {
   215			return FailureModeUnknown
   216		}
   217		if exitCode != 0 {
   218			return FailureModeUnknown
   219		}
   220		return ""
   221	}
